note:
一家名为国际合规性工作坊（ICW）的年轻公司决心在完成新一轮融资后，使全球公司的采购变得更加容易。联合创始人兼首席执行官林国荣（Garry Lam）告诉TechCrunch，这家香港初创公司刚刚完成了575万美元的A轮融资，将其总资金增加至约1000万美元。
 ICW就像供应商和买家的媒人一样，但是与现有的选择（例如阿里巴巴的B2B平台或国际贸易展览会）不同，ICW还在审查供应商的合规性，产品质量和认证。它会将所有这些信息收集到不断发展的数据库中，该数据库包含40,000多家供应商，其中80％目前在中国，并根据个人需求将其推荐给客户。
  ICW成立于2016年，目前的客户群包括一些世界上最大的零售商，包括Ralph Lauren，Prenatal零售集团，Blokker，Kmart和一家拒绝透露名字的美国大型药房连锁店。
 ICW的最新一轮融资由Infinity Ventures Partners牵头，Integrated Capital和现有投资者MindWorks Capital以及香港政府的20亿美元创新技术风险基金参与了该项目。
  由于中美贸易战和中国日益增长的劳动力成本，在国外采购的不断转移与顺应，ICW看到更多的客户实现了其供应链的多元化。但是过渡在短期内有局限性。
 “观察到某些产品类别的供应商仍然很困难，例如在其他国家/地区的蓝牙设备和移动电源，” Lam说道。 “但是对于服装和纺织品，这种转变已经在十年前开始发生。”
 在已经取代大量中国制造业活动的东南亚，每个国家都有其专门的领域。林说，越南有很多木制家具供应商，泰国以塑料制品闻名，马来西亚是医疗用品的好来源。 
当涉及到诸如人权制裁之类的棘手合规负担时，ICW依靠第三方认证机构来筛选和验证供应商。  “有一种[标准]资格标准可以验证供应商是否履行了企业社会责任……就像工厂是否遵守了劳动法，最低劳动权利或工资单一样，” Lam解释说。  ICW计划使用新收益来进一步开发其产品，包括其合规性管理系统，产品测试平台和B2B采购站点。
note_en:
One young company called  International Compliance Workshop, or ICW, is determined to make sourcing easier for companies around the world as it completed a fresh round of funding. The Hong Kong-based startup has just raised $5.75 million as part of its Series A round, boosting its total funding to around $10 million, co-founder and CEO Garry Lam told TechCrunch.
 ICW works like a matchmaker for suppliers and buyers, but unlike existing options like Alibaba’s B2B platform or international trade shows, ICW also vets suppliers over compliance, product quality, and accreditation. It gathers all that information into its growing database of over 40,000 suppliers — 80% of which are currently in China — and recommends them to customers based on individual needs.
  Founded in 2016, ICW’s current client base includes some of the world’s largest retailers, including Ralph Lauren, Prenatal Retail Group, Blokker, Kmart, and a major American pharmacy chain that declined to be named.
 ICW’s latest funding round was led by Infinity Ventures Partners with participation from Integrated Capital and existing investors MindWorks Capital and the Hong Kong government’s $2 billion  Innovation and Technology Venture Fund.
  In line with the ongoing shift of sourcing outside China, in part due to the U.S.-China trade war and China’s growing labor costs, ICW has seen more customers diversifying their supply chains. But the transition has limitations in the short run.
 “It’s still very difficult to find suppliers of certain product categories, for example, Bluetooth devices and power banks, in other countries,” observed Lam. “But for garment and textile, the transition already began to happen a decade ago.”
 In Southeast Asia, which has been replacing a great deal of Chinese manufacturing activity, each country has its slight specialization. Whereas Vietnam abounds with wooden furniture suppliers, Thailand is known for plastic goods and Malaysia is a good source for medical supplies, said Lam.
 When it comes to trickier compliance burdens, such as human rights sanctions, ICW relies on third-party certification institutes to screen and verify suppliers.
 “There is a [type of] qualification standard that verifies whether a supplier has fulfilled its corporate social responsibility… like whether the factory fulfills the labor law, the minimum labor rights, or the payroll, everything,” Lam explained.
 ICW plans to use the fresh proceeds to further develop its products, including its compliance management system, product testing platform, and B2B sourcing site.
title: 贸易战中香港初创公司ICW着眼于供应链多元化需求
title_en: Hong Kong startup ICW eyes supply chain diversification demand amid trade war

note:
Epic Games Store发布了一系列统计数据，以展示其游戏店面在2020年与发布年2019年相比的增长情况。该公司宣布，目前PC上的帐户已超过1.6亿，而2019年的注册用户为1.08亿。月活跃用户几乎翻了一番，从2019年12月的3200万增加到上个月的5600万。 Epic还看到其每日活跃用户数量增加到3130万，该公司称这增加了192％。 2020年，其峰值同时在线玩家人数达到1300万，几乎是其2019年700万峰值的两倍。
 毫不奇怪，每周免费赠送免费游戏是一种让人们回到您的店面的好方法。 （如果您的商店是Fortnite的PC唯一供应商，这也将有所帮助。）去年共有103款免费游戏，游戏玩家声称获得了7.49亿份拷贝。好消息：Epic承诺在2021年继续延续这种流行的赠送头衔传统。
  所有这一切都表明Epic Games Store正在增长。 2020年，游戏玩家在PC游戏商店中花费了7亿美元，其中2.65亿美元是游戏玩家在非Epic制作的第三方游戏上的实际支出（不包括这些游戏所使用的优惠券和促销的价值以及Epic自己为这些开发商提供的资金的价值）。
 展望未来，Epic表示，它正在努力使开发人员更容易在Epic Games Store上发布游戏。它说对其他所有人来说，它正在开发面向社区的功能，包括对其社交组件，玩家个人资料以及成就和愿望清单功能的更新进行全面检查。也许，它将继续努力举办更多游戏，而不仅仅是游戏。
 除非在接下来的10年中出现一些完全不可预测的趋势变化，否则Steam的销量将永远超过Epic Games Store。但它在2020年的强劲表现证明该行业中还有另一个竞争对手可以保持增长的空间。似乎可以证明，Epic的策略与Valve的策略不同（在限时游戏中进行交易，每次游戏购买都要向开发商支付更多费用）仍在引起开发商和发行商的共鸣。
note_en:
Epic Games Store released a  bevy of stats to show off how its game storefront grew in 2020 compared to 2019, its launch year. It announced that there are over 160 million accounts now on PC, up from the  108 million users registered in 2019. It almost doubled in monthly active users from 32 million in December 2019 to 56 million last month. Epic also saw its daily active user count go up to 31.3 million, which the company claims is a 192 percent boost. Its peak concurrent player count hit 13 million in 2020, almost doubling its 7 million peak in 2019.
 Unsurprising to no one, giving away free games each week is a nice way to keep people coming back to your storefront. (It also helps when your store is the sole provider of  Fortnite on PC.) There were 103 free games last year, and gamers claimed over 749 million copies. And good news: Epic has committed to continuing this popular tradition of giving away titles in 2021.
  All of this to say that the Epic Games Store is growing. Gamers spent $700 million within the PC game store in 2020, with $265 million of that being the actual amount spent by gamers on third-party games not made by Epic (excluding the value of coupons and promos used on those games, as well as the value of Epic’s own funding of these developers).
 Looking forward, Epic says it’s working to make it easier for developers to publish games on the Epic Games Store. For everyone else, it says it’s working on community-oriented features, including an overhaul to its social components, player profiles, and updates to its achievements and wishlist features. Perhaps, it’ll expand on its efforts to host  more than just games, too.
 Barring some totally unpredictable shift in tide in the next 10 years, Steam will always have bigger numbers than the Epic Games Store. But its solid performance in 2020 proves that there’s room in the industry for another competitor to keep growing. And it seems to validate that the ways Epic’s strategy differs from Valve’s (making deals for timed-exclusive games, paying developers more per game purchase) are still resonating with developers and publishers.
title: Epic表示其游戏商店拥有1.6亿+ PC用户，高于2019年的1.08亿，DAU同比增长192％至3,130万; 2020年，游戏玩家花费了7亿美元以上，其中3.65亿美元用于第三方游戏
title_en: Epic says its Games Store has 160M+ PC users, up from 108M in 2019, with DAUs up 192% YoY to 31.3M; gamers spent $700M+ in 2020, with $265M on 3rd-party games
